name: Satvik Diet Principles
description: Foundational yogic dietary guidance emphasizing wholesome, pleasant, steadying food that supports clarity and healing.
instructions: Favor fresh, light, nourishing, sattva-promoting food patterns and reduce rajasic / tamasic aggravators.
notes: Based on sattvika ahara references used across digestive and positive-health sections.

name: Low-Fat / Low-Cholesterol Cardiac Diet Guidance
description: Cardiac-supportive diet guidance emphasizing low saturated fat and low cholesterol intake.
instructions: Use low-fat, low-cholesterol nutrition planning and reduce known cardiovascular dietary risk factors.
notes: Supported by explicit cardiac / PAD references to low-fat, low-cholesterol diet.

name: Balanced Diabetes Diet Guidance
description: Balanced food guidance for metabolic regulation and diabetes support.
instructions: Use a balanced, digestible, glucose-aware food routine that supports metabolic steadiness and avoids overloading.
notes: Aligned with laghu ahara and diabetes-supportive dietary framing.

name: Obesity Low-Calorie Balanced Diet Guidance
description: Structured food guidance for obesity management through calorie awareness and lighter balanced intake.
instructions: Prefer balanced low-calorie meals, mindful eating, and avoid overeating patterns linked with stress and inactivity.
notes: Supported by obesity sections and honey/lime-water, cabbage, and dietary-reduction discussions.

name: Asthma Dietary Discipline
description: Warm, light, non-aggravating dietary discipline for kapha-heavy respiratory patterns.
instructions: Favor warm, light, easy-to-digest foods and reduce kapha-aggravating, mucus-promoting food habits.
notes: Backed by explicit asthma food discipline and kapha-management references.

name: Back Pain Supportive Diet Guidance
description: Supportive diet and routine guidance for lowering inflammatory load and aiding recovery in back-pain care.
instructions: Use simple nourishing meals, good hydration, and avoid food habits that increase heaviness or recovery burden.
notes: Curated as a practical supportive lifestyle item for musculoskeletal home guidance.

name: Depression Food and Stimulant Avoidance Guidance
description: Diet and habit guidance to reduce food and stimulant patterns that aggravate mood disturbance and poor sleep.
instructions: Reduce alcohol, smoking, overstimulation, and erratic eating habits that worsen emotional instability and fatigue.
notes: Derived from repeated anxiety/depression and psychosomatic lifestyle references.

name: Laghu Ahara Guidance
description: Light, easily digestible diet guidance used in digestive and metabolic support contexts.
instructions: Use easily digestible, lighter meals such as simple rice/khichadi-type patterns when therapeutic lightness is needed.
notes: Explicitly described as useful in fever, obesity, and diabetes; also valuable in digestive regulation.

name: Viruddhahara Avoidance
description: Guidance to avoid incompatible food combinations that disturb digestion and recovery.
instructions: Reduce incompatible food combinations and apply simple compatible-meal planning to support digestion.
notes: Directly backed by ayurvedic viruddhahara references.

name: Stress-Free Lifestyle Guidance
description: Daily-life regulation guidance aimed at reducing chronic tension, reactivity, and psychosomatic load.
instructions: Create a steadier rhythm with adequate rest, reduced overstimulation, and daily decompression practices.
notes: Supported by repeated “tension free life style” recommendations across common ailments sections.

name: Yuktahara-Vihara Regulation
description: Balanced regulation of food, movement, sleep, and work-rest patterns based on yukta living.
instructions: Balance food, recreation, activity, work, sleep, and waking habits to restore steadiness and reduce disease load.
notes: Directly grounded in “yuktahara viharasya yukta cestasya karmasu”.

name: Smoking Cessation Support
description: Therapeutic behavior-change guidance to stop smoking and reduce smoking-linked disease burden.
instructions: Use an explicit smoking-cessation plan and ongoing support to reduce cardiovascular and respiratory risk.
notes: Strongly supported by repeated “stop smoking” recommendations in cardiac, PAD, respiratory, and risk-reduction sections.
